1.description: Social welfare. Solid line: Social welfare as a function of Sector 1 size K1 using the social-welfare-maximizing emission cap, given K1. Dashed line: Social welfare without emission caps, U^(1,K1,1−K1)⁠, as a function of Sector 1 size K1. Parameters: γ1=γ2=α1=α2=0.5⁠, δ = 6.

2.description: SRF’s performance as a function of the SRF’s size S and strategy. panel a) Mere exclusion strategy where K2FB:=1−KO⁠, panel b) Scope 1 strategy, panel c) upstream Scope 3 strategy. Parameters are: γ1=γ2=α1=α2=0.5⁠, δ = 6, ψ=0.025,λ=0.5⁠.

3.description: SRF size and impact as a function of the fraction θ of value-alignment investors. Three strategies: mere exclusion (solid lines), Scope 1 (dotted line), and Upstream Scope 3 (dashed line). These graphs are for δ = 6, μ=0.005⁠, and ψ=0.025,λ=0.5 with capital market frictions in both sectors. Furthermore, for panels a) and b), α1=α2=γ1=γ2=0.5⁠; for panels c) and d), α1=0.1,α2=0.8,γ1=0.2,γ2=0.8⁠.

4.description: SRF size and impact. Size (panel a) and impact (panel b) as a function of the fraction θ of value-alignment investors for three strategies: mere exclusion (Solid lines), Scope 1 (dashed line), Upstream Scope 3 (dotted line), Downtream Scope 3 (gray dashed line), and Degrowth (gray solid line). These graphs are obtained for α1=α2=γ1=γ2=λ=0.5⁠, δ = 6, μ=0.005⁠, and ψ=0.025,λ=0.5,ρ=0.05 with capital market frictions in both sectors.
